---
title: "vdpau black screen (monitor off) in mythTV"
date: 2012-09-17
forum: Mythbuntu
---

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-09-17
I have a nettop (Revo with ION) and if I enable vdpau in playback in mythbuntu, my monitor actually turns off.  Any other non-vdpau playback profile works (some better than others), but the CPU goes crazy.

I upgraded from Maverick to Natty and I still have the same issue.  I was reading different things about Maverick, so I thought I would give the upgrade a try.

I tried to install the nvidia ppa, but I think it's having issues.  When I apt-get update, it gives a 404 error when it tries to hit that server.  ppa:nvidia-vdpau/ppa

I also tried the x-swat, which seems to update, but I still don't get the updated nvidia drivers.

I have nvidia-current installed.  However, it doesn't say what version it is in the "additional drivers" gui.  In the Nvidia X Server Settings GUI, it references 304.43.  I was hoping for 195 or 260 or one of the versions that people always talk about.

Can someone help me figure this out?  Please let me know what I can provide to assist in this.

Thank you so much.

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-09-17
> **davidstoll said:**
> I have a nettop (Revo with ION) and if I enable vdpau in playback in mythbuntu, my monitor actually turns off.  At what point does it turn off? I assume you mean when you start to play a video, rather than at the point you change the setting?

Is X actually crashing? Does the menus etc come back when the video stops playing?

What do your frontend logs and X log (/var/log/Xorg.0.log) say at the point when the screen goes blank?> Any other non-vdpau playback profile works (some better than others), but the CPU goes crazy.

I upgraded from Maverick to Natty and I still have the same issue.  I was reading different things about Maverick, so I thought I would give the upgrade a try.

I tried to install the nvidia ppa, but I think it's having issues.  When I apt-get update, it gives a 404 error when it tries to hit that server.  ppa:nvidia-vdpau/ppa

I also tried the x-swat, which seems to update, but I still don't get the updated nvidia drivers.

I have nvidia-current installed.  However, it doesn't say what version it is in the "additional drivers" gui.  In the Nvidia X Server Settings GUI, it references 304.43.  I was hoping for 195 or 260 or one of the versions that people always talk about.

Can someone help me figure this out?  Please let me know what I can provide to assist in this.

Thank you so much.304.43 is the latest available from nvidia so don't expect downstream packagers to provide you with anything newer! However I am not sure why you want to go outside the version provided for your distro which would be 270.41 [http://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=nvidia-current&searchon=names&suite=all&section=all](http://packages.ubuntu.com/search?keywords=nvidia-current&searchon=names&suite=all&section=all)

My lucid revo machines run 195.36 which works fine.

To get your version of the drivers type ```
dpkg -l nvidia*
```

### Post by nickrout on 2012-09-17
I am not sure about that Xorg log entry, it looks like X is trying to change to 24fps and failing. Are you trying to play a 24fps video? The 79124.780 would tell me that this is quite some time after your machine started up (pretty sure that is 79000 seconds). That would be a funny time to be setting itself up.

Look at Xorg.0.log before and after playing something and see if it changes - that'll tell you if that specific entry is related to playback or setup. Or you can tail -f /var/log/Xorg.0.log for a live performance. (Best way to do this is via ssh from another machine, preferably a laptop so you can sit on the sofa, play a video and observe your TV and the ssh session at the same time.) 

What about your frontend log at playback time?

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-09-28
Looking at post #3 or #4

The OP does not look to have the libvdpau installed.
The vdpau library is not part of the nvidia driver package since 2009.

Makes sense to install vdpauinfo (as well).
Run "vdpauinfo" in a terminal before & after installing libvdpau1.

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-09-28
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> Looking at post #3 or #4

The OP does not look to have the libvdpau installed.

what makes you say that? The package is NOT nividia-libvdpau, it is libvdpau1. The output in post #4 will not show libvdpau.

But worth checking nonetheless.

### Post by nickrout on 2012-09-28
I posted on the mailing list re this problem and got this suggestion> Review the xorg.conf of both the good and bad atom machines.  The bad one may be pushing the TV beyond it's limits for refresh rate or resolution.

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-09-28
Nick's right, post #3 did not show libvdpau1..

Weren't the revo/ion boxes very picky about the RAM configuration/type?
Can you change the RAM split between system & GPU ?

Could download/compile "qvdpautest"
[https://github.com/robertmassaioli/qvdpautest](https://github.com/robertmassaioli/qvdpautest)
But a vdpau decode failure would show in the MythTV logs..

So it's probably the display's reported EDID..

If you think the video mode needs investigating then set
Option "ModeDebug"  "True"
in /etc/X11/xorg.conf..logout/login.

Try different HDMI jack on TV (limited reliable specs on your TV on web).
I know you are using DVI/HDMI..
FYI The VGA jack supports 1080p only at 60Hz (59.9) reduced v blank timing.
There are no 50Hz modes stated as supported over VGA.

---

apt-get remove mythtv-frontend
apt-get install mythtv-frontend

### Post by nickrout on 2012-09-29
Just slightly embarrassed I had forgotten about it...

Take a look at your playback profiles and see if vdpaucolorspace is mentioned and try getting rid of it - that's what I took out of the other thread.

removing and reinstalling the frontend package will not change the playback profiles - they are all stored in the database.

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-09-29
vdpaustudio (level) has been removed as well, controlled from the OSD GUI..

Why should un-used keywords cause such problems..rhetorical..

---

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-09-29
> **nickrout said:**
> Just slightly embarrassed I had forgotten about it...

Take a look at your playback profiles and see if vdpaucolorspace is mentioned and try getting rid of it - that's what I took out of the other thread.

removing and reinstalling the frontend package will not change the playback profiles - they are all stored in the database.


ok, good to go!  All vdpaucolorspace=auto has been removed from all 3 VDPAU playback profiles (slim, normal, high), and they all work.

Sorry for asking a question that has already been resolved, but I guess it sometimes depends on the key words you use to search for it...I didn't know what to call it, so...

Anyway, thanks!

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-07
Have you been bumped back to the nouveau driver after the update?

Have a look in /var/log/Xorg.0.log..

start the FE from terminal
mythfrontend -v all

then do what every makes the screen go blank/black/off.

try <alt>+<tab> to change focus to terminal or exit FE if you have to.

---

> OK so what options are there in your profiles? in the place where there used to be vdpaucolorspace, what is in that box now?

Try mythfrontend -v playback, it'll give you some more clues.

NOT mythfrontend.real either!

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-08
Try switching from the QT painter to openGL.
I thought some of the themes did not like QT..

I take it that the posted stdout playback log did not involve any screen blanking ??

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-11-08
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> Try switching from the QT painter to openGL.
I thought some of the themes did not like QT..

I take it that the posted stdout playback log did not involve any screen blanking ??

The screen is blanking out and just like before, I can hear the audio.  I also changed to Opengl in the appearance option menu, but it didn't make a difference (the above dump is using opengl, whereas the previous dump used Qt).

The theme I am using is the same one I've always used and never changed the painter.  It was always whatever is default (Qt, I guess).

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-10
Are there any clues in /var/log/Xorg.0.log that show up at the same time as the screen goes blank/off?

Do you have the Myth fe set up to use different video mode for GUI & to playback?

You posted previous that this TV worked okay with another revo-ION box?
Suggests there must be a residual config file or myth database setting causing this problem.

When you use nvidia-settings:
- do all the listed available video modes actually work when you select them?
- does the 1080 @ 24p actually work ?

Your TV manual does not explain any of the important stuff about what is supported over HDMI & how it handles 24p..

The information in post #3 could be the clue..

---

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-11-10
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> Are there any clues in /var/log/Xorg.0.log that show up at the same time as the screen goes blank/off?

Do you have the Myth fe set up to use different video mode for GUI & to playback?

You posted previous that this TV worked okay with another revo-ION box?
Suggests there must be a residual config file or myth database setting causing this problem.

When you use nvidia-settings:
- do all the listed available video modes actually work when you select them?

The file /var/log/Xorg.0.log doesn't change after I reproduce the problem.  No extra lines are added to the log file.  I even closed and reopened it.

I do not have the FE setup to use a different video mode for Gui vs playback.

The TV is fine.  This nettop worked just fine after I removed those old filters.  Then, I upgraded to 12.04 and it started to have the same issue, and the filters are not there.

I've never used anything else but 1920x1080.  But I did try a couple other common resolutions 1280x720, etc.  I tried about 4 others and they "work" when I select them.  If you mean that the tv/driver/pc works with those resolutions, then yes.  Also, I noticed that when I did that the Xorg.0.log file changed....so I know if something was going to get written to that file when I tried to play the file, I would have seen it.

:(

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-10
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> Is the RAM setup the same as the other working Revo box ?

How is your audio output setup ?
HDMI audio to TV as stereo PCM or AC3 ?
Maybe this upsets the TV..
Using the MythTV AC3 encoder ?
Looks like we are all clutching at the same straws here...

I think David should take a very close look at his playback profiles. For each machine (ie one that works and the one that doesn't, both with same hardware, the following mysql might assist.

```
select * from displayprofilegroups where hostname = 'lounge';
+---------------------+----------+----------------+
| name                | hostname | profilegroupid |
+---------------------+----------+----------------+
| High Quality        | lounge   |            141 |
| Normal              | lounge   |            142 |
| Slim                | lounge   |            143 |
| VDPAU High Quality  | lounge   |            144 |
| VDPAU Normal        | lounge   |            145 |
| VDPAU Slim          | lounge   |            146 |
| OpenGL Slim         | lounge   |            188 |
| OpenGL Normal       | lounge   |            187 |
| OpenGL High Quality | lounge   |            186 |
+---------------------+----------+----------------+
9 rows in set (0.00 sec)

```

As you see this will give you a set of playback profile names and corresponding profilegroupids. Note the profilegroupid for the profile name that you are using, say it's VDPAU Normal which is number 145. Then:

```
select * from displayprofiles where profilegroupid=145;
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
| profilegroupid | profileid | value              | data                    |
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
|            145 |       471 | pref_priority      | 1                       |
|            145 |       471 | pref_cmp0          | >= 0 720                |
|            145 |       471 | pref_decoder       | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       471 | pref_max_cpus      | 1                       |
|            145 |       471 | pref_skiploop      | 1                       |
|            145 |       471 | pref_videorenderer | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       471 | pref_osdrenderer   | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       471 | pref_osdfade       | 1                       |
|            145 |       471 | pref_deint0        | vdpaubasicdoublerate    |
|            145 |       471 | pref_deint1        | vdpaubasic              |
|            145 |       471 | pref_filters       | vdpaucolorspace=auto    |
|            145 |       472 | pref_priority      | 2                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_cmp0          | > 0 0                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_decoder       | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_max_cpus      | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_skiploop      | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_videorenderer | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_osdrenderer   | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_osdfade       | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_deint0        | vdpauadvanceddoublerate |
|            145 |       472 | pref_deint1        | vdpauadvanced           |
|            145 |       472 | pref_filters       | vdpaucolorspace=auto    |
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
22 rows in set (0.00 sec)

```

This will give you a list of the settings for that profile which you can compare to the other machine and see if there are any differences. You could in fact output the settings to two files (profile,working and profile.notworking) and then use diff to compare them.

---

```

As you see this will give you a set of playback profile names and corresponding profilegroupids. Note the profilegroupid for the profile name that you are using, say it's VDPAU Normal which is number 145. Then:

```
select * from displayprofiles where profilegroupid=145;
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
| profilegroupid | profileid | value              | data                    |
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
|            145 |       471 | pref_priority      | 1                       |
|            145 |       471 | pref_cmp0          | >= 0 720                |
|            145 |       471 | pref_decoder       | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       471 | pref_max_cpus      | 1                       |

|            145 |       471 | pref_filters       | vdpaucolorspace=auto    |
|            145 |       472 | pref_priority      | 2                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_cmp0          | > 0 0                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_decoder       | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_max_cpus      | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_skiploop      | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_videorenderer | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_osdrenderer   | vdpau                   |
|            145 |       472 | pref_osdfade       | 1                       |
|            145 |       472 | pref_deint0        | vdpauadvanceddoublerate |
|            145 |       472 | pref_deint1        | vdpauadvanced           |
|            145 |       472 | pref_filters       | vdpaucolorspace=auto    |
+----------------+-----------+--------------------+-------------------------+
22 rows in set (0.00 sec)

```

This will give you a list of the settings for that profile which you can compare to the other machine and see if there are any differences. You could in fact output the settings to two files (profile,working and profile.notworking) and then use diff to compare them.

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-11
> **davidstoll said:**
> I'm not sure where to input these select statements.Sorry I assumed too much mysql knowledge.

run the following at the commandline and you will get a mysql prompt:

```
mysql -h media -p -u mythtv mythconverg
```

where media is the name of my backend, substitute your own. It wil prompt you for a password which is the database password from ~/.mythtv/config.xml or mysql.txt.

Then you can enter the suggested statements. The ; at the end of each line is needed. ctrl-d to exit mysql. 

If you want the results paged like when you use less, type ```
\P less
``` at the mysql prompt (although the results from my suggested queries should be short enough to see on one page).

---

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-11-12
I can do this for the backend/frontend machine, but not on the frontend.  Basically, I can't get into the mysql prompt for the separate frontend machine.  I used the correct host (even just tried 127.0.0.1) with the same mysql/myth database password.

---

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-12
From a terminal is this any easier ?

Assuming user mythtv & password mythtv:
```

echo "select select * from displayprofilegroups;" | mysql -N -umythtv -pmythtv mythconverg

```

Eyeball the listed hostnames, find the target..
(for example "mymaster")

```

echo "select select * from displayprofilegroups where hostname = 'mymaster';" | mysql -N -umythtv -pmythtv mythconverg

```

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-12
> **davidstoll said:**
> I can do this for the backend/frontend machine, but not on the frontend.  Basically, I can't get into the mysql prompt for the separate frontend machine.  I used the correct host (even just tried 127.0.0.1) with the same mysql/myth database password.The database is on the backend machine, it is not replicated on the frontend. You can get all the information you need from the prompt that works.

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-15
Those comparisons are for "vdpau normal"..I thought you were using "vdpau slim"..

The additional db entry "pref_skiploop" relates to the setting (tick box) in video playback profile screen enable/disable deblocking loop filter for H264 video.

Weak GPU/CPU setups could need to enable (tick) to skip the loopfilter.

---

The additional db entry "pref_skiploop" relates to the setting (tick box) in video playback profile screen enable/disable deblocking loop filter for H264 video.

Weak GPU/CPU setups could need to enable (tick) to skip the loopfilter.

dpkg -l nvidia-current
```

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-16
could also add this to /etc/X11/xorg.conf:
Option "ModeDebug" "true"
(put into any section in file but server section makes sense)

might increase the info around any mode changes into /var/log/Xorg.0.log.

Are you using an DVI-->hdmi adapter/cable ?

Section "ServerLayout"

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-18
In a terminal:
mythfrontend -v playback

then induce the problem..
exit & review the stdout.

Could try:
mythfrontend -v all

The "all" option is a bit OTT.

If you have syslogging setup you could:
mythfrontend --syslog -v all

then review the /var/log/mythtv/*.log files..

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-19
When you start mythfrontend from terminal (without --syslog) then all logging goes to stdout & not to syslog ..

Is xscreensaver installed in mythbuntu?
Should be okay I use xscreensaver with mythtv on lucid works..
No libCEC (prob a good thing)..

Why does you log show XVideo blit rendering?

Your PC seems to be trying to use XV blit rendering with vdpau decoding which is not allowed/supported..
XV XVideo is also deprecated AFAIK & no one should have been using it for years.

Please change your video playback profile by:
- clicking on "vdpau slim"
- click edit (to get to new screen)
- make sure vdpau is set as **render** & decoder 

You had better check all your vdpau playback profiles & make sure they all use vdpau rendering..

> When you start mythfrontend from terminal (without --syslog) then all logging goes to stdout & not to syslog ..

No libCEC (prob a good thing)..

Why does you log show XVideo blit rendering?

Your PC seems to be trying to use XV blit rendering with vdpau decoding which is not allowed/supported..
XV XVideo is also deprecated AFAIK & no one should have been using it for years.

Please change your video playback profile by:
- clicking on "vdpau slim"
- click edit (to get to new screen)
- make sure vdpau is set as **render** & decoder 

You had better check all your vdpau playback profiles & make sure they all use vdpau rendering..

xscreensaver was installed...hmmm...well anyway, it's removed now.

vdpau slim is now the playback profile.  It has been the playback profile before, but in my troubleshooting, I change it while I try other things.  I was able to confirm that vdpau is the render and decoder.  All the vdpau profiles are the same in that respect.  However, all of the vdpau and non-vdpau based playback profiles have the same issue of a black screen with audio upon playback or live tv.

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-20
There is no problem with xscreensaver on my lucid box & mythtv..
The problems were with gnome screensaver..

Your log file clearly shows:
VDPAU profiles 42 43 46 47 50 51 being ignored because of the "not supported" selected ffmpeg decoder.

XVideo is being used as fullback device because VDP vdpau render can not be used with your ffmpeg decoder.

VDPAU rendering is supported with s/w decoding & h/w decode with ffmpegvdpau codecs..

Are you building mythtv from source ?
Have you tried to build install ffmpeg libav* libraries ?

Is it possible you have replaced your system shared libav* libraries ?

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-20
Early in this thread there was discussion about other ppa..
That suggests the xbmc might not be from std repos..

So the distro package managed version of mythtv in mythbuntu has self-contained ffmpeg libav* ?
And I'm not talking about mythffmpeg..
[after checking std packages ..this does not have dependency on libav]

So mythbuntu is just Xubuntu with a local static build of mythtv installed into /usr/bin & a some scripts + control centre?

I would be very surprised if mythtv package in std ubuntu repos is built that way.
But it would seem a bit crazy to have a static build of mythffmpeg & then use shared libraries for libav*..
[after checking std packages ..this does not have dependency on libav]

The project split of ffmpeg & ubuntu then choosing to go with libav project could have forced mythtv package in *buntu to be self-contained.

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-20
> **BicyclerBoy said:**
> Early in this thread there was discussion about other ppa..
That suggests the xbmc might not be from std repos..

So the distro package managed version of mythtv in mythbuntu has self-contained ffmpeg libav* ?
And I'm not talking about mythffmpeg..
[after checking std packages ..this does not have dependency on libav]

So mythbuntu is just Xubuntu with a local static build of mythtv installed into /usr/bin & a some scripts + control centre?no it is not a static build, the mythtv devs import ffmpeg source into their own tree and then modify it for their own purposes/bug fixes. This is the same for all mythtv - whether it is packaged or built from scratch. Therefore it doesn't matter what libav or ffmpeg you have installed on the system, because mythtv never uses them.> 

I would be very surprised if mythtv package in std ubuntu repos is built that way.
But it would seem a bit crazy to have a static build of mythffmpeg & then use shared libraries for libav*..
[after checking std packages ..this does not have dependency on libav]

The project split of ffmpeg & ubuntu then choosing to go with libav project could have forced mythtv package in *buntu to be self-contained.

### Post by BicyclerBoy on 2012-11-21
Like Nick stated..mythtv should be unaffected by /immune to external changes to ffmpeg/libav packages.

I wasn't sure about mythbuntu distro.. 

I don't see anthing wrong with your latest postings..
Would install the libav*-extras packages (replaces the non-extras packages) if you need avconv-ffmpeg to be useful..

All I can imagine is:
- there is sometime wrong/hidden with video playback profile
- something messed up in mythconverg database
- some mythtv version mismatch schema mismatch between FE BE
- failed schema update.

I would try the potentially unsafe complete removal of this slave frontend from the database & a reinstall of frontend.
Possibly a fresh install of the frontend with a new hostname could be sufficient..

You have an (unrelated) db table missing warning with mythweather plugin..could be you have just never run it (from the menu).

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-21
Another option go to Settings|General Settings|Page two.

Choose (tick) Use custom identifier for frontend preferences. And give it a unique name. It should then start with a fresh and uncorrupted set of preferences. (once mythfrontend is restarted).

---

### Post by davidstoll on 2012-11-21
> **nickrout said:**
> Another option go to Settings|General Settings|Page two.

Choose (tick) Use custom identifier for frontend preferences. And give it a unique name. It should then start with a fresh and uncorrupted set of preferences. (once mythfrontend is restarted).

Well, that fixed it!  I had to do some re-configuring of some of the settings, sound, theme, shutdown command, etc, but all in all, it was only a couple of minutes to set it back up the way I had it.

Like the instructions say, the custom identifier name must be different than the network name of the device.  I also got a crash popup.  When I restarted the the frontend (program), it didn't seem to take.  Not sure if it was because of the crash or if the machine (rather than the program) needed to be restarted, but anyway...

Wow, that was a strange one.  Same exact problem (from the user perspective), but totally different fixes...in the same thread.

Thanks again nickrout and BicyclerBoy for your help and patience with me.  I'm still learning this, but, in part, because of this forum, I have not installed Winblow$ on a machine in a couple of years.

Is there a place to make a paypal donation to the forum or ubuntu or ...?

Thanks again guys for all your help, you are the best!

I'll mark the thread as solved...again.

---

### Post by nickrout on 2012-11-21
Yeah obviously there was something assed up in your settings - which could have been in the settings table, or (more likely in my humble opinion) the profiles tables. So both times it was in fact the same problem (a setting wrong) but in the first case it was easier to find and correct the setting. In the second case we just blew away all your settings and went back to the defaults.

If you really wanted to look into it further you could analyse those tables to death and see what has changed between old and new, but it's probably more for your own curiosity than anything else. I am curious about it still, but not curious enough to spend too much time :)

---
